transcript: Good afternoon everyone. Very excited to be here and to tell you a bit more about the Tala story in the next five minutes. And this exciting real-world use case that we're actually bringing on-chain as we speak this week. My name is Nicolas Cabrera, I'm the Chief Product Officer of this company called Tala. And I've been a builder like many, many of us here in the space. Either developing smart wallet solutions or money movement solutions. Really trying to change and evolve the financial infrastructure. And all of those solutions are designed to help democratizing finance. Yeah? Many of us have been on this journey. Um, all smart solutions, leveraging new technology, blockchain, stablecoins, AI. But often what's missing in this mission of democratizing finance is the true connections and the engagement with people that need it the most. Yeah? And that's where Tala comes in. Tala's been running for about 10 years now. And really focusing on the global majority users. People living in emerging markets. Mexico and LatAm, countries in Africa, Southeast Asia, India. And people that have been overlooked and unbanked by the traditional financial systems. Yeah? Those users don't have a credit score. And that's what Tala has been doing and providing unsecured lending, credit solutions to those people that they need the most. We do it in a very unique way. We developed a proprietary underwriting and decisioning solutions. Collecting a ton of data from those users that don't have a more structured dataset that sometimes we are used to in, um, in more kind of Western worlds. And because of that they don't have access that maybe some of us have here. And that's what Tala has been developing at scale, as you can see. We're operating in more than six markets now. LatAm, Africa, Southeast Asia. More markets coming up and live very soon. We've disbursed about 7 billion US dollars worth of loan, unsecured loans. Uh, and why we've been able to do this, limiting the fraud and the risk, is because of our proprietary data underwriting and decisioning engine. We've done it in the off-chain world for some time. And now we're really excited to bring that on-chain for the efficiency that obviously tokenization and on-chain finance can bring. And the combining our access, our engagement to those 13 million customers all around the world that need this access to on-chain finance and on-chain services, combined with the technology and that we can bring with Solana and some of our partners, um, we believe we're gonna really make a big impact to our customers' life. Our platform that we've been developing for the last few years is really plug-and-play. So from raising capital in a more traditional way on capital markets to underwriting, data collection and underwriting for those customers, all the way to disbursement of those loans and the on-off rails from disbursing the loan. Our customers need that liquidity in their everyday life. So the off-ramping is very critical for our customers. This week, we're very excited to bring a lot of this data, 10 years of history of those customers, we estimate it's about 4 billion customers of the global majority, and bringing that on-chain with the partnership that we announced with Solana and Huma Finance. What does it look like? You know, we're gonna establish a 50 million dollar global debt facility denominated in USDC. That's gonna allow us to originate the loans on-chain. And the value of that, I don't need to tell all of you, is that we're gonna be able to tokenize the loan, each loan or a pool of loans, and expose those loans to new traders. Retail, institutional traders. And that's where we work with our partner Huma Finance to do so. That's gonna generate new source and new pool of liquidity that our customers would benefit from. Yeah? Um, we're gonna disburse the loan in USDC, going forward maybe in local stablecoins as well. And we're gonna be able to do that end-to-end on-chain lending solutions directly to our own customers or exposing that to some of our partners in the Solana ecosystem that wants to expose an unsecured loan on-chain to your customers. Very exciting. Um, we know the benefit, you know, that's, uh, it's gonna bring. We bring a ton of history and data, unique dataset about those customers that most of us here try to connect and evolve their access. Um, it's gonna be bringing new, more efficient capital. And then longer term, more than just access to capital and everything that the on-chain finance journey will provide to our customers, it's also this concept of identity. That for the first time we're gonna create a unique identity on-chain to the global majority customers that will be able to carry that with them for the first time and then be served in new ways by Tala directly in some of the service that we're providing and the broader community as well. So more than just money movement, we're bringing identity and lending on-chain to all of our customers. Pretty exciting. Um, if you want to connect with us, would love to partner. There are many ways we can partner, bringing some new form of liquidity, uh, exposing your services to our 13 million customers. And please reach out. Thank you.

# Announcing Windows 11 Insider Preview Build 26220.7344 (Dev & Beta Channels)
Written By
* [Windows Insider Program Team](https://blogs.windows.com/windows-insider/author/windows-insider-program-team/)publishedDecember 5, 2025
Hello Windows Insiders, today we are releasing**Windows 11 Insider Preview Build 26220.7344**(KB5070316) to the Dev & Beta Channels.
As a reminder, we are offering the same builds to both the Dev & Beta Channels on Windows 11, version 25H2.
If you are an Insider in the Dev Channel, you now have a window to switch from the Dev Channel to the Beta Channel if you would like. This window will only be open for as long as we’re releasing the same 25H2-based updates across both the Dev and Beta Channels. After we move Dev Channel forward to a higher build number, the opportunity to switch between these channels will close. When the Dev Channel jumps ahead, things might not be as stable as the Dev Channel is today, so we highly encourage you to evaluate which channel you would like to be in during the time in which the window to switch is open.
Changes in Dev && Beta Channel builds and updates are documented in two buckets: new features, improvements, and fixes that are being gradually rolled out for Insiders who have turned on the toggle to get the latest updates as they are available (via Settings >> Windows Update\*) and then new features, improvements, and fixes rolling out to everyone in the Dev && Beta Channels.**For more information, see the Reminders section at the bottom of this blog post.**
## **New features gradually being rolled out with toggle on\***
### Public preview of native support for MCP on Windows
Windows now offers native support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that gives AI agents a universal way to connect with apps, tools, and services. Agents can discover and connect to these tools and other agents via a secure, manageable Windows****on-device registry (ODR). By default, all agent connectors in the Windows on-device registry will be contained in a secure environment with their own identity and audit trail. With OS level integration, MCP on Windows delivers secure discoverability, consistent controls, and enterprise grade manageability—empowering developers, IT professionals, and end users to build and deploy

# Fara-7B:An Efficient Agentic Model forComputer Use
PublishedNovember 24, 2025
By[Ahmed Awadallah](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/hassanam/),Partner Research Manager[Akshay Nambi](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/akshayn/),Principal Researcher[Alexey Taymanov](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/ataymano/),Principal Software Engineer[Aravind Rajeswaran](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/arrajeswaran/),Principal Researcher[Corby Rosset](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/corbyrosset/),Senior Researcher[Hussein Mozannar](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/hmozannar/),Senior Researcher[Spencer Whitehead](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/spwhitehead/)[Vibhav Vineet](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/vivineet/),Principal Researcher[Yash Lara](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/yashlara/),Senior PM[Yash Pandya](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/yashpandya/),Senior Research Engineer[Andrew Zhao](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/people/andrewzhao/),Applied Scientist

* [Share on Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/fara-7b-an-efficient-agentic-model-for-computer-use/)
* [Share on X](<
https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Fara-7B: An Efficient Agentic Model for Computer Use&url=https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/fara-7b-an-efficient-agentic-model-for-computer-use/ >)

* [Share on Reddit](<
http://www.reddit.com/submit?title=Fara-7B: An Efficient Agentic Model for Computer Use&url=https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/fara-7b-an-efficient-agentic-model-for-computer-use/ >)
* [Subscribe to our RSS feed](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/feed/)
### Pushing the frontiers of computer-use agents with an open-weight, ultra-compact model,optimizedfor real-world web tasks
![Three white line icons on a blue-to-green gradient background: a computer monitor with a globe symbol on the left, a cursor arrow with click lines in the center, and a computer mouse outline on the right.](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Fara7B-BlogHeroFeature-1400x788_NEW-scaled.jpg)
In 2024,Microsoftintroduced small language models (SLMs) to customers, starting with the release of[Phi(opens in new tab)](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/phi)models on[MicrosoftFoundry(opens in new tab)](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/products/ai-foundry),aswell as deploying[Phi Silica(opens in new tab)](https://blogs.windows.com/windowsexperience/2024/12/06/phi-silica-small-but-mighty-on-device-slm/)on Copilot+PCspowered by Windows 11. Today, we are pleased toannounce**Fara-7B**, our first**agentic SLM**designed specifically forcomputeruse.
Unlike traditional chat models that generate text-based responses, ComputerUse Agent (CUA) models like Fara-7Bleverage computer interfaces, such as a mouseand keyboard, to complete tasks on behalf of users. With only 7 billion parameters, Fara-7Bachievesstate-of-the-artperformance within its size class and is competitive with larger, more resource-intensive agentic systems that depend on

tags.impact_reason: Tala bringing $7B in unsecured loans on-chain via Solana could have a significant impact on real-world asset adoption and DeFi accessibility for emerging markets.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a worn, handwritten loan document being transformed into a glowing data crystal as the Semantic Anchor. This visually represents the core story: tokenizing real-world, unsecured credit for emerging markets (the paper) into a sophisticated financial asset on the Solana blockchain (the crystal). It captures the essence of 'RWA'. I included the text '$50M' as a holographic data point on the crystal's surface because it's the specific, high-impact figure from the headline, grounding the abstract concept in the tangible scale of the deal and fitting the diegetic text requirement.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot of a worn, slightly crumpled paper loan document resting on a dark, polished surface. A brilliant beam of cyan and purple light scans across the paper, and where the light touches, the paper's fibers transform into a glowing, translucent data crystal. The crystal is mid-formation, still connected to the organic paper. On the newly formed crystal facet, the text '$50M' glows as a soft, holographic data overlay. The background is a deep, out-of-focus datascape with faint light trails suggesting global network connections.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental macro shot, composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, showcases a heavily worn and subtly crumpled paper loan document. The paper, exhibiting crisp folds and visible matte grain texture, rests on a dark, highly polished surface. A brilliant, dramatic beam of cyan and purple volumetric light scans across the document, acting as a transformative force, indicative of an alchemical process. Where the vibrant light touches, the paper's organic fibers are captured mid-transformation, exquisitely forming into a glowing, intricate translucent data crystal. This nascent crystal, still organically connected to the original paper, exhibits internal light scattering, detailed refractive qualities, and a holographic glitch effect. On one of the newly formed crystalline facets, the characters '$50M' glow with a soft, ethereal holographic data overlay, appearing physically embedded within the crystal's surface. The background is a deeply out-of-focus datascape, rendered with a dark cyberpunk urban blur, featuring faint, subtle light trails that suggest vast, interconnected global network pathways, adding to an epic scale and leaving significant negative space above the subject. The lighting is a blend of dramatic one-beam lighting and soft neon mixed lighting, utilizing High Dynamic Range (HDR) and Global Illumination, resulting in soft blended neon shadows and cinematic contrast. The overall aesthetic is hyperrealistic and photorealistic, emphasizing every ultra-detailed facet and realistic material. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, Unreal Engine 5, volumetric lighting, HDR.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a worn, handwritten loan document being transformed into a glowing data crystal as the Semantic Anchor. This visually represents the core story: tokenizing real-world, unsecured credit for emerging markets (the paper) into a sophisticated financial asset on the Solana blockchain (the crystal). It captures the essence of 'RWA'. I included the text '$50M' as a holographic data point on the crystal's surface because it's the specific, high-impact figure from the headline, grounding the abstract concept in the tangible scale of the deal and fitting the diegetic text requirement.
visual_idea.description: An extreme close-up focusing on the point of transformation. We see the textured, fibrous strands of the aged paper document seamlessly merging and recrystallizing into the sharp, geometric, and internally luminous facets of a Solana-hued data block. The lighting is dramatic and singular, coming from the unseen scanning beam, casting micro-shadows and highlighting the textural contrast between the organic paper and the perfect digital crystal.

final_prompt.prompt_text: An extreme close-up focusing on the point of transformation. The textured, fibrous strands of an aged paper document seamlessly merge and recrystallize into the sharp, geometric, and internally luminous facets of a Solana-hued data block. Dramatic, singular one-beam lighting, characteristic of a scanning beam, casts intricate micro-shadows and intensely highlights the profound textural contrast between the organic, worn paper and the perfect, pristine digital crystal. The translucent data crystal facets shimmer with subtle holographic glitch effects, capturing light with ray-traced optics. The composition is a perfectly symmetrical, centrally focused tight macro shot, meticulously filling the entire 1:1 square frame with rich texture and hyper-detailed clarity. Hyperrealistic, photoreal detail, 8K resolution, octane render, physically based rendering (PBR), volumetric lighting, High Dynamic Range (HDR), cinematic contrast, deep blacks, vibrant Solana-hued accents, ultra-detailed, Unreal Engine 5.

editorial.headline: Fintech Giant Tala Deploys $50M USDC Debt Facility on Solana
editorial.standfirst: The 10-year-old lending platform partners with Huma Finance to tokenize unsecured credit for 13 million emerging market users, signaling a shift from RWA pilots to institutional-grade origination.
key_takeaways: Tala establishes a **$50 million debt facility** in USDC to originate and disburse loans entirely on-chain.
The integration uses **Huma Finance** to tokenize loan pools, allowing retail and institutional traders to fund emerging market credit.
Tala's proprietary underwriting engine—built on **$7 billion in historical disbursements**—will generate portable on-chain credit identities.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a significant move for the Real World Asset (RWA) sector, global fintech **Tala** has announced the migration of its lending infrastructure to **Solana**, backed by a $50 million debt facility denominated in **USDC**. Unlike the experimental pilots often seen in the RWA space, this integration leverages Tala’s decade of operation and 13 million existing customers across Mexico, India, the Philippines, and Kenya to bring high-volume, unsecured lending on-chain immediately.

The mechanism relies on a partnership with **Huma Finance**, a PayFi network specializing in on-chain liquidity for real-world payment flows. By tokenizing individual loans or pooled debt, Tala can now expose emerging market credit yields to a global network of liquidity providers. This structure bypasses the inefficiencies of traditional cross-border capital markets, allowing Tala to disburse USDC directly to unbanked users who previously lacked access to dollar-denominated liquidity.

The core alpha here is not just the liquidity, but the data. **Nicolas Cabrera**, Tala’s Chief Product Officer, revealed that the company is bringing its proprietary underwriting engine on-chain. This engine, trained on over $7 billion in loan disbursements, utilizes non-traditional data points to score users who lack formal credit histories. By anchoring this data on Solana, Tala is effectively creating a portable on-chain identity for the "global majority," transforming ephemeral repayment data into a permanent, composable financial asset for the borrower.

For institutional investors, this represents a new class of yield backed by high-frequency, granular repayment data rather than opaque off-chain assets. The move validates the **Solana** network's capacity to handle high-throughput financial operations and positions Huma Finance as a critical middleware layer for institutional credit. As Cabrera noted, the long-term play extends beyond lending; it is about establishing a sovereign financial identity for users that can eventually interface with the broader DeFi ecosystem.
